description: Universal status semantics shared across the Sentient OS ecosystem.
rule: Statuses define meaning before color. Project-specific color systems are not part of the Foundation layer.

statuses.name: Active
statuses.meaning: An item is currently engaged, selected, or in effect.
statuses.usage: Selected navigation items, active filters, and engaged operating states.

statuses.name: Running
statuses.usage: Live runtime jobs, agent execution, and in-progress system actions.

statuses.name: Completed
statuses.meaning: A process or item has finished successfully.
statuses.usage: Resolved work, finished flows, and successful runtime outcomes.

statuses.name: Paused
statuses.meaning: An item is intentionally halted but recoverable.
statuses.usage: Deferred jobs, suspended automations, and temporarily stopped work.

statuses.name: Blocked
statuses.meaning: An item cannot proceed because of a dependency or failure condition.
statuses.usage: Dependency stops, hard workflow blockers, and action-required failures.

statuses.name: Warning
statuses.meaning: An item needs attention soon but is not fully failed.
statuses.usage: Risk indicators, degraded states, and cautionary system signals.

statuses.name: Error
statuses.meaning: An item has failed or entered an invalid state.
statuses.usage: Failed requests, system faults, and invalid operations.

statuses.name: Draft
statuses.meaning: An item exists but is not yet finalized or published.
statuses.usage: Pre-release content, planning artifacts, and editable work in progress.

statuses.name: Archived
statuses.meaning: An item is preserved for reference and no longer active in primary workflows.
statuses.usage: Historical projects, retired records, and no-longer-operational entries.

semanticTones.name: Info
semanticTones.meaning: Informational activity or active operational state.

semanticTones.name: Danger
semanticTones.meaning: Critical issue, hard stop, or failed state.
